name: Andromeda
shortDescription: Andromeda is a princess in Greek mythology, known for her beauty and her rescue by Perseus from a sea monster.
description: Andromeda is a prominent figure in Greek mythology, primarily known for her role in the Perseus myth. She was the daughter of King Cepheus and Queen Cassiopeia of Aethiopia. Cassiopeia boasted that Andromeda was more beautiful than the Nereids, sea nymphs known for their exquisite beauty. This angered Poseidon, god of the sea, who punished Aethiopia by sending a sea monster, Cetus, to ravage the land. To appease Poseidon and save their kingdom, Cepheus and Cassiopeia were instructed by an oracle to chain Andromeda to a rock as a sacrifice to the monster. It was in this vulnerable state that Perseus, returning from his quest to slay Medusa, encountered Andromeda. Struck by her beauty and plight, Perseus slew Cetus and rescued Andromeda. Following her rescue, Andromeda married Perseus and accompanied him on his travels. She bore him several children, including Perses, who was considered the ancestor of the Persians. After her death, Andromeda was placed among the stars as a constellation, forever immortalized in the night sky. The story of Andromeda represents themes of sacrifice, beauty, divine retribution, and the heroic intervention of Perseus. It highlights the consequences of hubris and the power of fate, as well as the triumph of courage and love over adversity.
